name: Managed Identity and RBAC Setup
description: Configure managed identities for Azure resources and assign role-based access control to eliminate credential management.
prerequisites: Owner or User Access Administrator role on the subscription

steps.title: Enable system-assigned managed identity
steps.command: az webapp identity assign --name myapp-unique --resource-group myapp-rg
steps.description: Creates a system-assigned managed identity tied to the lifecycle of the web app.
steps.tip: The output includes the principalId which you need for role assignments.

steps.title: Create a user-assigned managed identity
steps.command: az identity create --name myapp-identity --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus
steps.description: User-assigned identities can be shared across multiple resources.

steps.title: Assign a role to the identity
steps.command: az role assignment create --assignee <principalId> --role "Storage Blob Data Contributor" --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/myapp-rg
steps.description: Grants the managed identity permission to read and write blobs in the resource group.

steps.title: List role assignments
steps.description: Shows all roles assigned to the identity across scopes.

steps.title: Verify identity access
steps.command: az login --identity && az storage blob list --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --auth-mode login
steps.description: Tests that the managed identity can access the target resource.
steps.warning: This only works when run from the Azure resource that has the identity assigned.

fullScript:
# Managed identity and RBAC setup
az webapp identity assign --name myapp-unique --resource-group myapp-rg
az identity create --name myapp-identity --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus
# Replace <principalId> with the output from the identity assign command
az role assignment create --assignee <principalId> --role "Storage Blob Data Contributor" --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>/resourceGroups/myapp-rg
az role assignment list --assignee <principalId> --output table

faq.question: What is the difference between system-assigned and user-assigned managed identities?
faq.answer: System-assigned identities are tied to a single resource and deleted when the resource is deleted. User-assigned identities are standalone resources that can be attached to multiple services.

faq.question: Can managed identities access resources in other subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the identity is granted a role assignment at the target resource, resource group, or subscription scope.
